name: ISO 50001 Energy Baseline Calculator
description: Calculates the energy baseline and performance indicators per ISO 50001:2018, including normalization for production volume, weather, and other relevant variables.

inputs.label: Total Energy Consumption
inputs.businessContext: Total energy consumed by the organization in the baseline period (e.g., one year).

inputs.label: Production Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total production output in the baseline period (e.g., number of products, tons, or other relevant unit).

inputs.label: Heating Degree Days (HDD)
inputs.businessContext: Annual heating degree days for the facility location, used to normalize weather-related energy use.

inputs.label: Cooling Degree Days (CDD)
inputs.businessContext: Annual cooling degree days for the facility location, used to normalize weather-related energy use.

inputs.label: Operating Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total hours the facility operated during the baseline period.

inputs.label: Energy Source Mix
inputs.businessContext: Primary energy sources used, affecting emission factors and normalization.

inputs.label: Baseline Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of baseline per ISO 50001:2018 clause 6.3.

inputs.label: Apply Weather Normalization
inputs.businessContext: If true, adjusts energy consumption for heating and cooling degree days.

inputs.label: Apply Production Normalization
inputs.businessContext: If true, adjusts energy consumption for production volume.

rules.message: Production volume must be greater than zero when production normalization is enabled.
At least one of Heating Degree Days or Cooling Degree Days must be greater than zero when weather normalization is enabled.
Operating hours must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Energy intensity is more than 50% above baseline. Investigate potential energy waste.
Energy intensity has doubled compared to baseline. Immediate action required.
Production volume has dropped more than 20% from baseline. Re-evaluate baseline relevance.

formulas.description: Energy consumption adjusted for weather using degree days.
Energy consumption adjusted for production volume.
Energy intensity per unit of production (kWh/unit).
Baseline energy intensity calculated from historical data (assumed stored or input as reference).
EnPI = (Baseline Energy Intensity - Current Energy Intensity) / Baseline Energy Intensity * 100
Estimated cost savings based on energy reduction and average energy price (assumed $0.12/kWh).
Primary result: Energy Performance Indicator (EnPI) as a percentage improvement (positive = improvement).

primary.label: Energy Performance Indicator (EnPI)
primary.description: Percentage improvement (positive) or deterioration (negative) relative to baseline energy intensity.

production_normalized_energy.label: Production Normalized Energy
production_normalized_energy.description: Energy consumption per unit of production after weather normalization.

baseline_energy_intensity.label: Baseline Energy Intensity
baseline_energy_intensity.description: Reference energy intensity from baseline period.

total_energy_cost_savings.label: Total Energy Cost Savings
total_energy_cost_savings.description: Estimated cost savings compared to baseline energy consumption.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Weather Variation Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Energy change attributable to weather differences from baseline.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Production Variation Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Energy change attributable to production volume differences from baseline.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Residual Energy Change
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Energy change not explained by weather or production (e.g., efficiency changes, behavioral).

suggestedActions.label: Investigate residual energy increase
suggestedActions.description: If residual energy change is positive, conduct energy audits to identify waste sources.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize HVAC scheduling
suggestedActions.description: If weather impact is significant, review HVAC setpoints and schedules.

suggestedActions.label: Align production planning with energy tariffs
suggestedActions.description: Shift high-energy processes to off-peak hours to reduce costs.

suggestedActions.label: Implement energy monitoring system
suggestedActions.description: Install sub-metering to identify specific high-consumption areas.
